generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on the motor measuring angular displacement and velocity for the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot. Provides real-time data for backlash and positional uncertainty reduction."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted behind the gearbox measuring angular displacement and velocity for the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot. Used in conjunction with motor encoder to quantify backlash and positional inaccuracies."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring electrical input current (I_M) to the permanent magnet synchronous motor. Converts current to torque (τ_M) using manufacturer-provided motor constants."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
]

instance Sinumerik840DController : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller providing real-time access to position, speed, acceleration (actual and target values) for all axes via MQTT protocol."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ControllerDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance MotorCurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance ControllerDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom Sinumerik840DController
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance MotorDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable dynamics model converting motor current (I_M) to torque (τ_M) using manufacturer-provided parameters. Implemented in MATLAB Simulink."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkExecutionEnvironment
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box friction model implementing Coulomb-Viscous friction characteristics for the cycloidal gearbox. Used for simulating static and kinetic friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkExecutionEnvironment
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box friction model implementing Stribeck effect for the cycloidal gearbox, accounting for stiction and breakaway friction."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkExecutionEnvironment
]

instance DahlFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box friction model implementing Dahl model for the cycloidal gearbox, capturing nonlinear friction behavior."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkExecutionEnvironment
]

instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box friction model implementing LuGre model for the cycloidal gearbox, providing detailed simulation of friction dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkExecutionEnvironment
]

instance IdealGearboxSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink-based simulation modeling ideal behavior of the cycloidal gearbox without friction effects, providing reference outputs (δr_M, δr_G, δr_out)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkExecutionEnvironment
]

instance MeasuredMotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data representing measured motor current (I_M) transmitted via MQTT from the physical system."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MotorDynamicsModel
]

instance MeasuredEncoderData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data combining angular position and velocity measurements from both motor encoder (r_M) and secondary encoder (r_G)."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted, SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionModelComparisonEnabler
]

instance DerivedTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived torque values (τ_M, τ_G, τ_out) calculated from motor current (I_M) using manufacturer-provided dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionModelComparisonEnabler
]

instance SimulatedFrictionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated friction outputs (δr_M, δr_G, δr_out) generated by friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) in Simulink."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ControllerDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionModelComparisonEnabler
]

instance SystemPropertiesData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Contextual metadata including temperature, load conditions, and operational parameters linked to all data traces for environmental context."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ControllerDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationDashboard
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SimulinkExecutionEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB Simulink environment executing all friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) and component simulations for the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelingService, ControlOptimizationService
]

instance MQTTDataStreaming : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation enabling real-time streaming of sensor data (I_M, encoder readings, controller outputs) from physical system to digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataIntegrationService
]

instance SysMLModelingTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML 1.7 tool used for creating structural (BDDs) and behavioral (IBDs) models of the first axis components (motor, gearwheel, cycloidal gearbox)."
    DTDFVocab:enables StructuralModelingService
]

instance FrictionModelComparisonEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processing component comparing simulated friction model outputs (δr_M, δr_G) with measured data traces (r_M, r_G) to identify deviations."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelRefinementService, OperationalDiagnosticsService
]

instance VisualizationDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unified dashboard displaying real-time and historical data including torque-angular velocity plots, friction loss deviations, and operational diagnostics."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous identification and refinement of white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) friction models to quantify static, kinetic, and viscous friction within the gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionAnalysisInsight, ModelRefinementAction
]

instance ControlOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service deriving control parameters from friction modeling to inform model-based control algorithms that compensate for joint friction and improve positional accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlParameterAction
]

instance OperationalDiagnosticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service highlighting anomalies such as unexpected torque spikes or encoder discrepancies for predictive maintenance or calibration adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

instance DataIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service managing real-time data integration from physical sensors and controller via MQTT protocol into the digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTraceabilityInsight
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing unified visualization of real-time and historical data for operational monitoring and diagnostic purposes."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

instance StructuralModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service creating SysML-based structural models (BDDs and IBDs) describing the composition of the first axis components and their interconnections."
    DTDFVocab:provides AssetModelingInsight
]

instance ModelRefinementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service identifying deviations between simulated (δr_M, δr_G) and measured (r_M, r_G) data traces to guide iterative updates to friction models."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelUpdateAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative metrics for static, kinetic, and viscous friction losses in the gearbox, accounting for up to 30% of total motion inaccuracies."
]

instance ModelRefinementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Iterative updates to friction models based on deviations between simulated and measured data traces."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlParameterAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Updates to control parameters (e.g., PID gains) informed by friction modeling to compensate for joint friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of operational anomalies including unexpected torque spikes and encoder discrepancies for predictive maintenance."
]

instance DataTraceabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Traceability of data points back to their computational origins (simulation models, processing components) via metadata and associations."
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time and historical visualization of torque-angular velocity relationships and friction loss deviations for operational monitoring."
]

instance AssetModelingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structural representation of the first axis components (motor, gearwheel, cycloidal gearbox) and their interconnections via SysML models."
]

instance ModelUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic exchange of friction models during operation to adapt to wear, temperature, and load changes."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C1: Environment
instance MabiMax100FirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, GearWheelComponent, CycloidalGearboxComponent
]

instance DigitalShadowEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains SimulinkExecutionEnvironment, MQTTDataStreaming, SysMLModelingTool, FrictionModelComparisonEnabler, VisualizationDashboard
]

// C2: Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor driving the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot with maximum angular velocity of 75°/s within operational range [-90°, 90°]."
    base:isContainedIn MabiMax100FirstAxisEnvironment
]

instance GearWheelComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Gearwheel component in the transmission system of the first axis, transmitting rotational motion from motor to cycloidal gearbox."
    base:isContainedIn MabiMax100FirstAxisEnvironment
]

instance CycloidalGearboxComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox component providing high torque transmission and precision reduction for the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot."
    base:isContainedIn MabiMax100FirstAxisEnvironment
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital shadow operates in **real-time**, with continuous streaming of sensor data via MQTT and immediate processing of friction models. While exact data acquisition rates are not specified, the system is designed for **low-latency operation** (implied by 'live measurements' and 'real-time data-driven representations'). Visualization and analysis occur within **sub-second intervals** to support immediate insights for motion accuracy improvement."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation is orchestrated as a **purpose-specific, centralized digital shadow** with the following components:
   - **Core Components**:
     - **FrictionShadow (FrictionDerivation)**: Singleton class defining the purpose (friction analysis) and scope (first axis).
     - **Asset (Mabi)**: Represented via SysML SystemModel and simulation models (SimModel_M, SimModel_GW, SimModel_GB).
     - **Data Traces**: Organized into measured (e.g., I_M, r_M) and simulated (e.g., ˆr_G) data streams, each linked to its source (sensor, simulation).
     - **Processing Components**: Simulation and data calculation models (e.g., Coulomb-Viscous friction model) executed to generate derived data traces.
   - **Interactions**:
     - **Data Flow**: One-way from PT (via MQTT) to DT, with no feedback to the physical system.
     - **Traceability**: Enabled via metadata and associations in the DSRM, allowing data points to be traced back to their computational origins (e.g., simulation models, processing logic).
   - **Deployment**: The DT is implemented as a **software system** integrated with the robot’s control infrastructure (SINUMERIK 840D sl) but operates independently as a passive observer."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin covers the **operational phase** of the physical twin’s lifecycle, focusing on **as-operated** representation rather than design or manufacturing stages. Key aspects include:
   - **Purpose-driven development**: The DT is created to **improve motion accuracy** during operation by analyzing friction-induced deviations.
   - **Continuous evolution**: The DT is designed for **ongoing refinement** of friction models post-deployment, adapting to wear, temperature, and load changes over time.
   - **Scope**: Limited to the **first axis** of the MABI MAX 100 robot, with no explicit coverage of other lifecycle phases (e.g., design validation or manufacturing quality control)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The fidelity and validity of the digital twin are ensured through:
   - **Model Validation**:
     - **Simulink-based simulations** compare ideal behavior (without friction) against measured data to quantify deviations.
     - **Friction models** (e.g., Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck) are validated against empirical data to ensure accuracy in predicting torque losses.
   - **Uncertainty Handling**:
     - **Parameter identification**: Manufacturer-provided constants (e.g., motor torque coefficients) are used to derive torque from current (I_M).
     - **Encoder calibration**: Differences between motor and secondary encoders are analyzed to account for backlash and elasticity in the drive train.
   - **Verification Mechanisms**:
     - **Data traceability**: Each data point is linked to its source (sensor, simulation) and computational model, enabling auditable validation.
     - **Dashboard visualization**: Real-time comparison of simulated (ˆr_M, ˆr_G) vs. measured (r_M, r_G) data traces to identify discrepancies and validate model accuracy.
   - **Error Sources**:
     - **Measurement noise**: Sensor inaccuracies (e.g., encoder resolution) may introduce minor deviations.
     - **Model simplifications**: Analytical friction models may not capture all dynamic effects (e.g., temperature-dependent viscosity)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the physical twin (PT) and digital twin (DT) is established via:
   - **Network Protocol**: **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)** for real-time streaming of sensor data (e.g., motor current I_M, encoder readings) from the SINUMERIK 840D sl controller to the DT.
   - **Data Format**: Time-series data organized into structured **data traces** (e.g., arrays of torque τ and angular velocity ω) with metadata specifying source (e.g., encoder channel) and timestamp.
   - **Integration Points**:
     - **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl**: Acts as the intermediary between the PT and DT, providing access to control signals and sensor data.
     - **MQTT Broker**: Facilitates bidirectional communication (though the DT is passive), enabling low-latency data transmission.
   - **Latency**: Designed for **near-real-time synchronization** (sub-second delays) to ensure the DT reflects the PT’s current state."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital shadow is implemented as a **software system** deployed on an unspecified infrastructure. While the document does not specify the hosting environment (e.g., local server, cloud), it implies **on-premise deployment** due to the integration with the Siemens controller and real-time requirements. The system is designed for **executable deployment**, with visualization provided via a **dashboard interface**."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin adheres to the following standards and methodologies:
   - **Modeling Standards**:
     - **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)**: Used for structural and behavioral modeling of the first axis (compliant with SysML 1.7).
     - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: Implied as a potential standard for semantic interoperability (referenced in the abstract but not detailed).
   - **Data Exchange**:
     - **MQTT Protocol**: Industry-standard for IoT/industrial data streaming, ensuring compatibility with other systems.
   - **Simulation Standards**:
     - **MathWorks MATLAB Simulink**: Industry-leading tool for executable modeling and simulation, widely adopted in robotics and control engineering.
   - **Digital Twin Methodologies**:
     - **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)**: Provides a formal framework for structuring data traces, sources, and processing components.
     - **Purpose-driven approach**: Aligns with emerging digital twin methodologies for specific use-case optimization."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The document does not explicitly address security or safety considerations. However, implicit assumptions include:
   - **Passive Digital Shadow**: The DT does not execute autonomous actions on the PT, reducing risks of unintended physical interventions.
   - **Controlled Data Flow**: MQTT-based communication is secured via standard industrial practices (e.g., TLS encryption for sensitive data streams), though specifics are not provided.
   - **Safety Constraints**: The robot’s **safety cell** restricts the working range to [-90°, 90°] and maximum speed (75°/s), mitigating risks during operation.
   - **No Remote Operation**: The DT is designed as a **monitoring tool** and does not enable remote execution of hazardous tasks on the PT. Safety is managed by the SINUMERIK 840D sl controller under human supervision."
]
